title: Minor: member table copy-paste bug, exception clobbering, missing Py_VISIT
author: devdanzin
body:
Part of #348 (C extension analysis). [Full report](https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e8a25ea988dc0461c2fd378efd84a8a3).

Three small independent bugs, each a one-line fix.

## Finding 6: `encoder_members` maps `"encoding"` to wrong field

**Line 182.** Copy-paste from line 181: `offsetof(PyEncoderObject, encoder)` instead of `offsetof(PyEncoderObject, encoding)`. Accessing `.encoding` returns the encoder callable instead of the encoding value.

**Reproducer:**
```python
import simplejson._speedups as sp
import decimal

c_enc = sp.make_encoder(
    {}, None, sp.encode_basestring_ascii, None, ", ", ": ",
    False, False, True, None, False, False, False, None,
    None, "utf-8", False, False, decimal.Decimal, False,
)
print(f"c_enc.encoding = {c_enc.encoding!r}")
# <built-in function encode_basestring_ascii>  ← WRONG, should be "utf-8"
print(c_enc.encoding is c_enc.encoder)
# True — both return the same object!
```

**Fix:** Change `offsetof(PyEncoderObject, encoder)` to `offsetof(PyEncoderObject, encoding)` on line 182.

---

## Finding 12: `PyLong_AsLong` exception clobbering

**Line ~2595.** `PyLong_AsLong(int_as_string_bitcount)` returns -1 with `OverflowError` when the value overflows a C `long`. No `PyErr_Occurred()` check. The -1 fails the `> 0` check, falling to `PyErr_Format(TypeError, "int_as_string_bitcount (-1) must be greater than 0...")` which clobbers the real `OverflowError`.

**Reproducer:**
```python
import simplejson._speedups as sp
import decimal

sp.make_encoder(
    {}, None, sp.encode_basestring_ascii, None, ", ", ": ",
    False, False, True, None, False, False, False,
    2**100,  # overflows long
    None, "utf-8", False, False, decimal.Decimal, False,
)
# Actual:   TypeError: int_as_string_bitcount (-1) must be greater than 0...
# Expected: OverflowError
```

**Fix:** Add `if (val == -1 && PyErr_Occurred()) goto bail;` after `PyLong_AsLong`.

---

## Finding 5: `encoder_traverse` missing `Py_VISIT(skipkeys_bool)`

**Lines 3213-3234.** `encoder_clear` calls `Py_CLEAR(s->skipkeys_bool)` but `encoder_traverse` doesn't visit it. GC cycle detection gap. Low practical impact since `skipkeys_bool` is normally `True`/`False` (immortal objects).

**Fix:** Add `Py_VISIT(s->skipkeys_bool)` to `encoder_traverse`.
